anomaly_probe.anomaly_candidate: Huangbo's search-refusal, Rinzai or huatou great doubt, direct-recognition traditions, and gradual paths that also use strong support.
anomaly_probe.confidence_effect: Lowers confidence. The model should be treated as a coding hypothesis, not a doctrine. It must include no-question, question-as-medicine, direct-recognition, practice-realization, vow-held, bridge, mixed-by-stage, and analyst-frame-refused values.
anomaly_probe.why_it_breaks_or_strains_model: Huangbo may reject the analyst's attempt to classify support as guard or bridge. Huatou uses a question or doubt as medicine, so distance-making can itself be the practice. Direct recognition may require very specific teacher-held authorization. Gradual paths may share the same warnings and supports, making the distance field redundant.

claim_units: Some paths treat seeker-sought distance as real enough to cross, while others diagnose that distance as the illness.
Continuity-ecology and support-location coding should begin with a distance field.
In no-distance teachings, teacher, vow, posture, community, text, and conduct function as guards rather than bridges.
Modern solo users may mistake denied metaphysical distance for permission to abandon supports.
Adding a distance field should improve prediction of warnings, repairs, and failure clusters beyond tradition label or general support coding.

cross_domain_prediction.prediction: In ACT, behavioral activation, coaching, and training, models that frame the goal as already enacted through valued action will need guards against passivity and self-certainty, while models that frame the goal as distant skill acquisition will need bridges, scaffolds, and milestones.
cross_domain_prediction.structural_translation: Before prescribing supports, determine whether the change model treats the desired state as a later achievement or as a currently enacted relation that must be protected.
cross_domain_prediction.would_count_against_it: If bridge-style supports and guard-style supports do not differ in warnings, failure modes, or repair instructions across nonreligious change domains, the distance field is too thin.

exact_claim: Before coding where support is placed in a contemplative path, first determine whether the path permits a real distance between seeker and sought. In no-distance systems, supports are not bridges to a later prize but guards against rebuilding separation as private certainty, passivity, or isolation; no-distance is not no-support.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Held-out manuals and teacher interviews from Dogen, Shinran, Dzogchen, Mahamudra, and Chan will warn against private certainty, passivity, calculation, or isolation more than against insufficient effort toward a distant goal.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: No-distance texts use supports as guards of present realization, trust, conduct, or non-separation rather than bridges to later possession.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Split-source blind coding of primary texts, manuals, ethical rules, and practitioner interviews.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Held-out warning and repair language is indistinguishable from ordinary instrumental path warnings once tradition, teacher quality, practice intensity, and social support are controlled.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Compared with guided practitioners, solo users more often report passivity disguised as freedom, withdrawal disguised as completion, contempt for practice, or private certainty without conduct repair.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Modern unguided users of no-distance teachings have distinctive failure clusters.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Screened practitioner survey, adverse-event interviews, app-user study, and teacher-reported case coding.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Guided and unguided users show the same failure clusters, or ordinary social support and mental-health risk predict outcomes better than no-distance exposure.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Coders using a distance field predict held-out warnings, repair instructions, and teaching-permission limits better than coders using support-holder fields alone.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Adding a distance field improves support-ecology prediction.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Inter-rater coding experiment with pre-registered comparison to support-holder-only and VCE-factor baselines.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: The distance field has no independent predictive value after adding tradition label, teacher style, institution, social support, and clinical risk.

literature_search_queries: "Dzogchen" direct introduction stabilization conduct vows guru reliance
"Shinran" Other Power no calculation self-power support

near_neighbors.difference: The candidate turns this into a general comparative precondition for support coding and a modern safety warning.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Strong downgrade. The no-distance thesis is old; the possible contribution is the distance-field coding test.
near_neighbors.overlap: Very close primary and scholarly prior for rejecting practice as a bridge to later realization while preserving disciplined practice.
near_neighbors.source: Dogen, Bendowa practice-realization sources, for example https://buddhismnow.com/2023/10/08/practice-within-realisation-dogen/ and Heine on realization-based language, https://www.mdpi.com/2077-1444/12/2/81

near_neighbors.difference: The candidate interprets these as guards against rebuilding a gap rather than bridges.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Moderate downgrade and strong anomaly pressure. It requires finer stage and genre coding.
near_neighbors.overlap: Close primary case for direct recognition with continuing guru reliance, vows, devotion, renunciation, conduct, and stabilization.
near_neighbors.source: Longchen Rabjam, Lama Yangtik Trekcho instruction, https://www.lotsawahouse.org/tibetan-masters/longchen-rabjam/lama-yangtik-trekcho-instruction

near_neighbors.difference: The candidate places Shinran under the distance-test frame, which may distort tariki if treated as generic no-distance.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Downgrades novelty and requires an analyst-frame-refused option.
near_neighbors.overlap: Close prior for support that is not self-owned effort or bridge-building but vow-held entrusting.

near_neighbors.difference: Huangbo may reject both bridge language and support-architecture language more radically than the candidate allows.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Important anomaly. It can collapse the model into too much architecture.
near_neighbors.overlap: Close primary prior for search-refusal and warning that seeking itself can miss the point.
near_neighbors.source: Huangbo, The Transmission of Mind, public PDF at https://wayofsuddenawakening.org/wp-content/uploads/2025/01/Huang-Po-The-Transmission-of-Mind.pdf

near_neighbors.difference: The candidate focuses on seeker-sought distance and contemplative support rather than practices as social virtues or rituals.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Moderate downgrade for the guard-not-bridge logic.
near_neighbors.overlap: Close structural prior for practices whose goods are internal to the practice rather than external results.
near_neighbors.source: MacIntyre, After Virtue, and non-instrumental practice theory.

near_neighbors.difference: VCE does not propose a distance field, but it may predict safety better than the distance field.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Downgrades practice-safety originality and defines a baseline.
near_neighbors.overlap: Close prior for teacher, community, worldview, appraisal, social support, and practice factors shaping meditation difficulties and remedies.
near_neighbors.source: Lindahl et al., Varieties of Contemplative Experience, https://journals.plos.org/plosone/article?id=10.1371/journal.pone.0176239, plus Cheetah House resources, https://www.cheetahhouse.org/faq

near_neighbors.difference: The candidate specifies no-distance teachings and support abandonment as one bypass route.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Moderate downgrade of the modern failure-mode claim.
near_neighbors.overlap: Close prior for using spiritual ideas or practices to avoid psychological needs, difficult emotions, relational repair, and ordinary responsibilities.
near_neighbors.source: Spiritual bypassing literature, for example Masters 2010 and Cashwell et al. 2011, plus overview at https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC8134675/

near_neighbors.difference: This candidate condenses them into a first-step distance gate for continuity-ecology coding.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Major internal downgrade. Keep as consolidation or codebook field unless split-source tests show added prediction.
near_neighbors.overlap: Extremely close internal prior for non-instrumental practice, no-question frames, no-distance support, support checks, and no-audit routing.
near_neighbors.source: Internal Lumenary records: Not Every Practice Is a Tool, Some Paths Refuse the Question of What Remains, The Question Can Make the Distance, Even No Path Needs Care, The Grid Stays Backstage, and No One Begins Alone.

next_loop_instructions: Do not publish as standalone doctrine. Cross-index with Not Every Practice Is a Tool, Some Paths Refuse the Question of What Remains, The Question Can Make the Distance, Even No Path Needs Care, The Grid Stays Backstage, and No One Begins Alone.

Build a distance-gate codebook with values for bridge, guard, expression, no-distance, mixed-by-stage, question-as-medicine, no-question frame, vow-held, practice-realization, direct-recognition, gradual crossing, inherited practice, analyst-frame-refused, and undecidable.
Use split-source blind coding. Code distance from doctrinal or instruction passages, then predict warnings, repairs, and failure modes from held-out manuals or practitioner interviews.
Do not let one source establish both no-distance and the predicted repair.

Compare predictive power against support-holder-only coding, VCE variables, teacher quality, social support, institution, practice intensity, clinical risk, spiritual-bypass literature, and ordinary action-planning baselines.

Close-read Dogen Bendowa or Genjokoan, Shinran Tannisho and letters, Longchenpa Trekcho, Huangbo, one Mahamudra direct-introduction source, one Rinzai or huatou source, and one gradual Theravada manual before public wording.
Interview practitioners for concrete cases of no-distance misuse, passivity, isolation, premature certainty, and repair.

Pilot Name What Holds You only with screened stable adults or older teens with support. Exclude acute crisis, severe depression, mania, psychosis, dissociation, addiction withdrawal, OCD or scrupulosity, unsafe authority, trauma activation, coercive workload, and cases requiring moral repair or clinical care.

Measure withdrawal, passivity, ordinary conduct, help-seeking, loneliness, shame, rumination, private certainty, support contact, and whether the prompt performs better than ordinary social-support or action-planning prompts.
novelty_adjustment: Lower novelty from 0.58 to 0.44. The no-distance and non-instrumental practice content is heavily anticipated by Dogen, Shinran, Dzogchen, Huangbo, practice theory, spiritual-bypass warnings, VCE, and prior Lumenary records. Retain as an extended coding refinement only if the distance field adds held-out predictive value.

practitioner_test.answers_that_reduce_originality: Originality falls if practitioners say the bridge versus guard distinction is standard no-attainment, practice-realization, Other Power, or direct-recognition teaching, or if warnings are predicted better by lineage, teacher quality, institution, practice intensity, or clinical risk.
practitioner_test.answers_that_support_contribution: Contribution rises if practitioners can blind-code distance status and if that field predicts concrete repair instructions, warnings, and solo-user risks better than support-holder coding alone.
questions: In your tradition, are supports better described as bridges to a later result, guards of present realization, expressions of trust, ordinary conduct, or something else?
Can you give a concrete case where a student used no-distance language to abandon necessary support, and how was it repaired?
Does the distance field predict warnings and repairs that your existing lineage categories do not already predict?
Where does this frame distort your tradition, especially in direct recognition, vow-held practice, huatou, or practice-realization?
relevant_practitioners: Soto Zen teachers and Dogen scholars
Advaita teachers familiar with direct recognition and self-inquiry
clinicians working with spiritual bypassing and meditation-related distress

primary_pair: Dogen, Bendowa and practice-realization passages, for example https://buddhismnow.com/2023/10/08/practice-within-realisation-dogen/, read against Longchen Rabjam's Trekcho instruction, https://www.lotsawahouse.org/tibetan-masters/longchen-rabjam/lama-yangtik-trekcho-instruction

Shinran's Other Power letter, https://shinranworks.com/letters/a-collection-of-letters-zensho-text/what-we-call-other-power/, read against Huangbo's search-refusal teachings, for example https://wayofsuddenawakening.org/wp-content/uploads/2025/01/Huang-Po-The-Transmission-of-Mind.pdf
primary_text_close_read.risk_of_overread: Moderate to high. The model may simply rename established non-instrumental practice, practice-realization, no-attainment, sudden enlightenment, direct recognition, and Other Power. It also risks treating no-distance traditions as one family despite major differences between Dogen, Shinran, Dzogchen, Huangbo, Advaita, and Mahamudra. Human-condition fit is plausible but must stay narrow: stable self-directed seekers using no-distance language to justify withdrawal, passivity, meaning-loss, or status detachment. It is not for acute loneliness without support, severe depression, mania, psychosis, dissociation, trauma activation, addiction withdrawal, OCD or scrupulosity, unsafe authority, or cases requiring moral repair or clinical care.
primary_text_close_read.secondary_literature_gap: Before promotion, read Dogen scholarship on practice-realization and shusho-itto, Steven Heine and other Dogen scholars, Shinran scholarship on tariki and shinjin, Dzogchen and Mahamudra scholarship on direct introduction and stabilization, Huangbo and Chan studies on sudden enlightenment and anti-seeking rhetoric, non-instrumental ritual theory, MacIntyre on practices, Asad and Lindbeck on tradition and grammar, VCE and Cheetah House safety literature, and spiritual bypassing literature.
primary_text_close_read.structural_observation: Dogen strongly supports the no-distance side because practice and realization are not cleanly sequential. But his no-distance is not free-floating: it is enacted through zazen, forms, rules, teacher lineage, and conduct. Longchen Rabjam also strains any simple no-support reading: direct introduction appears, but sustaining recognition, devotion, vows, guru reliance, and conduct remain. Shinran rejects self-power calculation and places efficacy in Other Power, yet pastoral warning and entrusting are still structured. Huangbo is the hardest pressure because search itself is suspect, but the teaching still occurs inside transmission and teacher-student address. The common pattern is not absence of support but refusal to make support into a self-owned bridge to a prize.

title: First Ask If There Is Distance
unlike_statement: Unlike Dogen, Shinran, Longchenpa, Huangbo, MacIntyre, VCE, spiritual-bypass literature, and prior Lumenary no-support and question-permission records, the separable claim is that a pre-registered seeker-sought distance field should improve prediction of held-out warning and repair language, especially for modern solo misuse of no-distance teachings.
